paper-type.title: paper types
paper-type.description.PLAIN: Plain paper is regular and thin, like in any regular printer. The paper is suitable for printing sketches, 'sketches' and files with little ink. It is not suitable for printing flat files, photos and graphic works due to low ink absorption capacity.
paper-type.description.COATED: Coated paper is a high-quality, thick, matte white paper with a special coating layer suitable for flat prints (with a lot of ink). The paper is suitable for printing graphic works and can also be developed on it thanks to its high quality.
paper-type.description.PHOTO: Photo paper is the highest quality, thick paper, it is semi-glossy (half glossy) and has a special coating layer suitable for developing photos and printing graphic works filled with ink. This paper is actually a dedicated paper for developing photos in a wide format.
paper-type.description.A4: A4 paper is completely regular A4 paper, and is suitable for printing documents and simple graphic works. This paper is not suitable for printing flat works (with a lot of ink) due to its reduced ability to absorb ink.
paper-type.description.A3: A3 paper is regular paper in A3 sizes, completely regular (like in any regular printer), and is suitable for printing documents, sketches and simple graphic works. This paper is not suitable for printing flat works (with a lot of ink) due to its reduced ability to absorb ink.
paper-type.description.A4160: The A4 (160g) paper is the highest quality, thick paper in A4 sizes. The paper is matte white (not glossy at all) and is coated with a special coating layer for a very high level of printing and is suitable for printing flat files, graphic works and developing photos.
paper-type.description.A3160: The A3 (160g) paper is the highest quality, thick paper in A3 sizes. The paper is matte white (not glossy at all) and is coated with a special coating layer for a very high level of printing and is suitable for printing flat files, graphic works and developing photos.
paper-type.description.A4Recycled: Recycled A4 paper with a fine texture, 120 gsm thickness, and a soft cream shade. Suitable for artistic printing of delicate graphic works.
paper-type.description.SM: Small 'stills' photo - dimensions 15.24 by 10.16 cm. Printed on a high-quality, modern CITIZEN printer.
paper-type.description.MD: Medium 'stills' photo - dimensions 17.78 by 12.7 cm. Printed on a high-quality, modern CITIZEN printer.
paper-type.description.LG: Large 'stills' photo - dimensions 20.32 by 15.24 cm. Printed on a high-quality, modern CITIZEN printer.

Infill percentage determines how much material the printer uses to fill the internal parts of the print.
This setting does not affect the visual appearance, only the strength of the print.
The recommended value is 20% – higher values increase the cost, so if you're unsure, it's best not to change it.

edit-instructions.step-11-title: How to convert to JPEG through Photoshop?
edit-instructions.step-11-intro: Now we'll convert the PDF file you saved through {{software}} to JPEG format through Photoshop, at 300 DPI resolution.
edit-instructions.step-11-why-before: Ultimately, printers only print pixels, and our printers print only one file type - JPEG, at 300 DPI resolution -
edit-instructions.step-11-why-bold: a very high resolution in which the pixels cannot be seen with the naked eye.
edit-instructions.step-11-other-before: Any other file (PDF, PNG, or JPEG at a resolution other than 300 DPI) is
edit-instructions.step-11-other-bold: converted to JPEG format at 300 DPI before being sent to the printer
edit-instructions.step-11-other-after: in a process you have no control over, which may cause unexpected printing problems and long processing times.
edit-instructions.step-11-step1: 1. Save the file as PDF from {{software}}.
edit-instructions.step-11-step2: 2. Open the PDF in Photoshop (or drag it into Photoshop).
edit-instructions.step-11-step3: 3. In the panel that opens, verify 4 settings:
edit-instructions.step-11-bullet1: Crop to is set to Media Box.
edit-instructions.step-11-then-ok: Then click OK, the panel closes, and save the file in JPEG format.
edit-instructions.step-12-ps-merge-intro: If the file contains more than one layer – we'll merge the layers:
edit-instructions.step-12-ps-merge-bulle: In the right panel, select Layers.
Select all layers together (you can click on the topmost layer, press Shift on the keyboard, then click on the bottommost layer).
Right-click with the mouse, and in the menu that opens select Merge Layers.
edit-instructions.step-12-jpeg-title: Now we'll save the file in JPEG format.
edit-instructions.step-12-pdf-title: Now we'll save the file in PDF format.
edit-instructions.step-12-ps-save-as: Click on the File tab and select Save as.
edit-instructions.step-12-ps-jpeg-save-t: In Save as type set JPEG (*.JPG,*.JPEG,*.JPE), choose a folder on your computer and click Save.
edit-instructions.step-12-ps-pdf-save-ty: In Save as type set Photoshop PDF (*.PDF,*.PDP), choose a folder on your computer and click Save.
edit-instructions.step-12-ps-save-then: Click Save and the file is saved in the folder you selected.

edit-instructions.step-12-ppt-pdf: Select PDF format, click "Save", and the file is saved in the folder you selected.
edit-instructions.step-12-word-file: In the top-left corner of the screen, click "File".
edit-instructions.step-12-word-print: Select "Print", select Microsoft Print to PDF, and click "Print".
edit-instructions.step-12-word-save: Verify the format is PDF, select a folder on your computer to save the file, click "Save", and the file is saved in the folder you selected.
edit-instructions.step-12-revit-export: Click on the File tab, select Export, and select PDF.
edit-instructions.step-12-revit-bullet1: In the Selected views/sheets field, All Sheets is selected. (You can select specific sheets only via the pen button on the right)
edit-instructions.step-12-revit-bullet2: In the Export Setup field, select PDF Export Setup Settings 1.
edit-instructions.step-12-revit-bullet3: In the File Name field, enter a name for the file.
edit-instructions.step-12-revit-bullet4: The Combine Selected Views and sheets into a single PDF file field is not checked (V).
edit-instructions.step-12-revit-bullet5: In the Location field, select a location on your computer to save the file.
edit-instructions.step-12-generic-title: Now we'll save the file for printing.
edit-instructions.step-12-question: Did you manage to save the file?
edit-instructions.step-12-antialias-intr: Anti-aliased is a mechanism for "edge smoothing".
edit-instructions.step-12-antialias-vect: When converting vectors (lines, fonts, shapes, etc.) to pixels for printing (as noted - the printer only prints pixels) - lines and shapes can have jagged edges - because pixels are squares.
edit-instructions.step-12-antialias-adds: Anti-aliased adds pixels in intermediate shades at the edge of the vector.
edit-instructions.step-12-antialias-off-: pixels are chosen to be the vector color or not in a "binary" way (yes or no).
Thin lines will always appear in print and won't disappear.
Lines may look stepped when merged diagonally.
edit-instructions.step-12-antialias-on-l: pixels get relative transparency in the vector color according to how much the vector covers the pixel.
edit-instructions.step-12-antialias-on-a: Line appearance in print will be smoother.
edit-instructions.step-12-antialias-on-d: Thin lines may disappear in print if they are not thick enough.
edit-instructions.step-12-antialias-reco: It is recommended to try converting the file with the Anti-aliased button checked (V), and to choose the pixel result in Photoshop up close.
edit-instructions.step-12-antialias-fix: Thin lines disappear or are fragmented? Thicken the lines, or convert again with the Anti-aliased button unchecked.
edit-instructions.step-13-title: What is important to check before printing?
edit-instructions.step-13-upload: Now we will upload the file to the interface.
edit-instructions.step-13-preview-bold: It is important to verify in the preview that the dimensions are correct.
edit-instructions.step-13-preview-note: Note that in the preview the image is not in original quality - it is a display-only image and we will send the file you uploaded for printing.

q-and-a.1.title: How to print with Eazix?
q-and-a.1.content-1: Welcome to Eazix! Here you can print directly from your personal device and collect your printout anytime you choose, easily and conveniently, from one of our self-service printing kiosks.
q-and-a.1.content-2: How to print with Eazix printers?
q-and-a.1.content-3: It's simple and easy! Choose a branch and a printer, upload a file and choose type of paper, send to print and pick your printing from one of our automatic printing kiosks spread across Israel, equipped with the most advanced printers on the market.
q-and-a.1.content-4: We have several types of printers:
q-and-a.1.content-plotter-1: Printing sketches, graphics, posters, and photo development in a wide format, i.e., on wide paper rolls of various types, through the most advanced printers from CANON.
q-and-a.1.content-express-1: Document printing in A4 and A3 sizes on various paper types using top-tier EPSON printers.
q-and-a.1.content-pics-1: Still photo development in 2 sizes using high-end CITIZEN printers.
q-and-a.2.title: How to edit a file for printing?
q-and-a.2.content-1: Properly editing a file for printing is extremely important if you want to achieve maximum print quality.
q-and-a.2.content-2: Therefore, we have prepared a detailed guide for proper file editing, available for {{count}} different software applications:
q-and-a.2.content-3: And in any case, if you didn't understand the instructions, or if you have any questions or issues during the process, a human representative will be available to assist you immediately, almost any time, so there's no need to worry, we're here for you all the time.
q-and-a.3.title: What are the opening hours of the branches?
q-and-a.3.content-1: The opening hours of the branches depend on the educational institution or business where the printer is located, so you should contact the institution or business directly to inquire about the opening hours.

choose-product.detailed-description.FDM: FDM technology uses a plastic filament that is heated and melted to build objects layer by layer. It is a common method with relatively low cost.
choose-product.suitable-for.FDM: Best for architectural models, prototypes, models for educational projects.
choose-product.detailed-description.MJF: MJF technology uses nylon powder to create strong, precise parts, with high geometric freedom that enables complex shapes.
choose-product.suitable-for.MJF: Best for functional parts, industrial production, accurate models.
choose-product.detailed-description.SLA: SLA technology uses liquid photopolymer resin ("resin") that is hardened by a laser beam, which allows creating very detailed models.
choose-product.suitable-for.SLA: Best for detailed models, jewelry, accurate prototypes.

tree-d-print.explain.1.content: Choose a printing technology from three technologies:
tree-d-print.explain.2.title: File upload, settings and pickup method
tree-d-print.explain.2.content: Upload a file in STL, OBJ, or 3MF format only, choose materials, color, quantity, and how to receive your order - from the factory, from our center, or with next-day delivery right to your doorstep. Also view our commitment to delivery times.
tree-d-print.explain.3.title: File verification
tree-d-print.explain.3.content: The file is checked by the machine operator at the factory and undergoes a printing simulation in the machine software. If we find problems, we will contact you directly through the dedicated chat in the interface, through which you can communicate directly with the machine operator at the factory.
tree-d-print.explain.4.title: Quote and payment
tree-d-print.explain.4.content: Receive a quote via SMS and in the interface, including price, location, and production and delivery times to which we commit. Approve and pay with a click.
tree-d-print.explain.5.title: Production and delivery
tree-d-print.explain.5.content: The work is produced at one of the factories, you receive an SMS notification when the work is ready. The work is delivered to you from our center, or with next-day delivery right to your doorstep.
tree-d-print.explain.6.title: Delivery confirmation and work quality
tree-d-print.explain.6.content: Have a problem? There's someone to talk to, as you know. You can contact us with any problem or question, and the machine operator will be happy to provide you with a quick and courteous response. Everything happens in one place - in the most transparent and convenient way.

product-type.name.canvas.canvas.exp: High-quality print on canvas fabric, stretched over a wooden frame. Print sizes according to your choice.

product-type.name.hard-materials.glass.e: Printing on glass—a clean, modern look; suitable for fronts, frames, and displays.
product-type.name.cup.straight.exp: Colorful print on a wide, straight ceramic mug. Suitable for hot and cold drinks.

product-type.name.plot.photo.exp: Very high-quality printing on glossy photo paper, ideal for photos and illustrations
product-type.name.plot.sketch.exp: Printing on thin sketch paper, suitable for architectural plans, drawings, and technical documents
product-type.name.plot.newpaper.exp: Thin, economical paper with a light grayish tone; suitable for sketches, drafts, and test prints

product-post-production.explain-blackCol: Black coloring of the 3D printed product.
product-post-production.explain-AMT: Smooth finishing (AMT) is a chemical process that enhances rough and porous MJF parts, turning them into parts with a smooth surface, improved sealing, and increased mechanical strength.
product-material.description.FDM-PLA: Easy to print, accurate, and cost-effective material. Ideal for models, prototypes, and decorative parts. Less resistant to heat and outdoor conditions.
product-material.description.FDM-PETG: Stronger and more durable than PLA, with slight flexibility and good moisture resistance. Suitable for functional parts and everyday use.
product-material.description.FDM-ABS: Heat-resistant and strong material, commonly used in industrial applications. Suitable for stressed parts and requires controlled printing conditions.
product-material.description.FDM-ASA: Similar to ABS but with excellent resistance to sunlight and outdoor conditions. Ideal for long-term outdoor use.
product-material.description.FDM-TPU: Flexible, rubber-like material. Ideal for elastic parts such as seals, protectors, and shock-absorbing components.
product-material.description.MJF-Nylon12: Strong and uniform industrial material with high resistance to wear and stress. Ideal for precise functional parts and serial production.
product-material.description.SLA-clearRe: Photopolymer resin ("resin"), highly accurate, with smooth finish and transparency option. Ideal for visual models and high-detail parts.
product-material.description.SLA-enginee: Photopolymer resin ("resin"), combining high precision with strong mechanical properties. Ideal for functional parts and engineering applications that require real load-bearing strength.
product-material.description.SLA-standar: Photopolymer resin ("resin"), combining high precision with basic strength. Suitable for visual models and prototypes, but less suited for engineering applications or load-bearing use. A more cost-effective choice when appearance matters more than strength.
